page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz with 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU.

This instance includes 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes directly attached to the hardware. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included with this instance type.

The inclusion of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage alongside a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space without the need for external storage provisioning. This hardware profile is configured for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, and applications requiring high-speed local storage and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by dedicated Intel processors. It delivers 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores operating at 3.9 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory. The instance features 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-speed local storage directly attached via the Nitro hypervisor. Networking capabilities include a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps. With a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU and substantial local storage, this instance is designed for general-purpose workloads, database hosting, and applications requiring high-speed local scratch space and high network throughput.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel processors, high-speed local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.

192.0 GB of system memory with a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2850 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
